story.title: Our story
story.body1: Born in 2005 in Riacho He Hé (Formosa), we built a warm, rigorous learning space for every age.
story.body2: We focus on dynamic, playful classes grounded in real-life situations so you use the language with confidence.

modalities.title: On-site & live online
modalities.intro: You can choose the format that fits your routine without giving up the same CEFR-based path, the Ministry of Education of Formosa recognition (Res. 1297/05), and our UTN and Cambridge preparation routes.

presencial.title: On-site classes
presencial.lead: Learn at our campus in Riacho He Hé: guided lessons, steady pacing, and the social energy of a real classroom.
presencial.b1: Small groups with close teacher support and clear speaking practice.
presencial.b2: Printed and digital materials that match each level of the framework.
presencial.b3: Community moments—presentations, screenings, and celebrations—with staff and classmates.

remota.title: Live distance learning
remota.lead: Study from home with 100% live sessions—no passive recordings—with the same objectives and follow-up as on-site courses.
remota.b1: Typically two live meetings per week (60 minutes), with steady instructor guidance.
remota.b2: Reduced groups, guided conversation, and international-standard materials.
remota.b3: Ideal if you want to save travel time while keeping a rigorous rhythm.

alts: Students in an on-site English class, seated at desks with the teacher at the front of the room.
Learners working during a lesson in the institute’s classroom.
Group of children smiling together during an in-person cultural activity at the center.
Students and a teacher celebrating exams with certificates in front of an institute banner.

studentGallery.title: Our students
studentGallery.intro: Tap an album to browse photos from class and celebrations.

levels.title: Study levels
levels.introTrail: : a path from A1 to C2. At C2, the framework describes its ceiling—performance across skills at 100% of the reference.
levels.scaleAria: Visual progression from A1 to C2 along a color band; C2 marks the top of the framework

certs.title: Credentials & pathways
certs.edu: Registered as No. 69 in the non-systemic education registry of the Private Education department. Ministerial Resolution No. 1297/05 Formosa.
certs.utn: You can also pursue national certification through our agreement with UTN Argentina.
certs.cambridge: We prepare students to sit the B2 First Certificate with Cambridge accreditation.
certs.altUtnIngles: English at UTN — National Higher Institute of Technical Teacher Training

import.title: Import students (CSV)
import.hint: Columns supported (Spanish or English headers): nombre/first_name, apellido/last_name, dni or documento, teléfono/phone, email, fecha_nacimiento/birth_date (YYYY-MM-DD), nivel/level (A1–C2), academic_year (default 2026), dni_tutor/tutor_dni, tutor_email, nombre_tutor/apellido_tutor, cuota/monthly_fee (optional). Creates parent account + links when tutor DNI is present. Seeds 12 pending payments for 2026. Password defaults to the student’s DNI (padded to 6 characters if needed).

import.importModalExplainBody: “Rows” means lines in your CSV. “New users” counts only new Auth accounts created for students who did not already exist. Existing students may only get profile updates, enrollments, or payments — so new users can be fewer than rows. Skipped rows usually mean no change was needed.

analytics.title: Analytics & usage
analytics.lead: Aggregated events (no identifiable personal data in metadata). Time zone: Argentina. Public traffic hits are logged on the server (middleware GET + User-Agent + edge country), so crawlers without JavaScript count as bots where the UA matches; signed-in users also emit product events separately.
analytics.trafficHint: Last 30 days: each GET under a locale path recorded at the edge (not the in-app beacon). Bots from User-Agent; guests have no session.
analytics.trafficCardsHint: Click Signed-in, Guests or Bots to see which URLs and user-agents make up the count.
analytics.trafficCardAriaAuth: Show top URLs and clients for signed-in visits
analytics.trafficCardAriaGuest: Show top URLs and clients for guest (no session) visits
analytics.trafficCardAriaBot: Show which bots are hitting the site and which URLs they fetch
analytics.trafficBreakdownPathsHint: Most visited pathnames for this visitor kind in the period.
analytics.trafficBreakdownPathsEmpty: No URL hits in this period.
analytics.trafficBreakdownAgentsHint: Most frequent User-Agent strings reported by these visitors.
analytics.trafficBreakdownAgentsEmpty: No User-Agent data available in this period.
analytics.trafficBreakdownBotsHint: Most frequent bot User-Agents detected in this period.
analytics.trafficBreakdownBotsEmpty: No bot traffic in this period.
analytics.trafficBreakdownGuestProtectedNotice: Some pathnames here normally require a session. They usually come from bots scanning common admin URLs (the layout redirected them to /login) or from signed-in users whose session cookie didn't reach the analytics ping.
analytics.tipTrafficStatTotal: All page loads in the last 30 days under a locale path, including guests and bots.
analytics.tipTrafficStatAuth: Loads where a signed-in session cookie was present (any role).
analytics.tipTrafficStatGuest: Loads from visitors without an authenticated session.
analytics.tipTrafficStatBot: Automated traffic classified from User-Agent (crawlers, monitors, etc.).
analytics.chartWorldMapHint: Last 30 days: page hits with country from edge headers (traffic_page_hits). Darker = more hits vs. the busiest country in this period.
analytics.trafficGeoPathHint: Last 30 days: top country + URL path pairs (hits with a known edge country). Capped list; order by hit count.
analytics.trafficGeoPathEmpty: No rows with country in this period.
analytics.trafficGuestPathHint: Last 30 days: top URL paths for anonymous visits only (visitor_kind guest). Signed-in users and bot-classified hits are excluded.
analytics.trafficGuestPathEmpty: No guest hits in this period.
analytics.chartWorldMapLegend: Intensity is relative to the top country in the range (not absolute population).
analytics.worldMapError: Could not load the map outline. Check your connection or try again later.
analytics.worldMapNoCountryData: No geolocated hits in this period. This is common in local development (localhost/private IP) where edge country headers are not available.
analytics.worldMapTooltipNoVisits: No page hits in this period for this country.
analytics.chartFunnelHint: Last 7 days: page_view events with the section: prefix (e.g. payments, messages).
analytics.chartGeoHint: From edge headers (e.g. Vercel); may show “unknown”.

health.title: Section health
health.lead: Headline metrics for active enrollments. Detailed editors live in the other tabs.
health.windowHint: Portal analytics counts (material views, learning area) use the last 30 days.
health.kpiAttendanceFoot: Present + late + excused over all marks in this section.

health.summaryScreenReader: Section summary: {students} active students, capacity utilization {capacity}, attendance rate {attendance}.
health.chartEmpty: Not enough data for this chart yet.
health.chartHelpAttendanceBody:
A single horizontal stacked bar shows how all attendance marks split across statuses (segment length is the raw count; the full bar is every mark in the section).

Counts come from the section_attendance table: for each active enrollment we count rows with each status (batched for performance).

The headline attendance rate in the summary strip uses (present + late + excused) divided by (present + absent + late + excused), rounded to a whole percent.
health.chartHelpTasksBody:
Radial bands show the same three counts as before: each arc is one progress bucket (not opened, opened, completed).

This classifies each student_task_progress row that joins active task instances for this section with your active enrollments.

Instances are non-archived task_instances for this section (capped on the server). Each progress status maps to Not opened, Opened, or Completed (including completed late).

If some enrollments do not yet have a row for every instance, row totals can be lower than instances times students.
health.chartHelpCapacityBody:
A treemap uses rectangle area proportional to each value: larger means more seats in that bucket.

It compares seats already filled by active students with seats still free up to the section effective capacity.

Filled seats equals the number of active enrollments. Free seats is effective capacity minus active students, never negative.

Capacity values come from the section configuration you maintain under Configuration.
health.chartHelpPaymentsBody:
A donut is the clearest part-to-whole view for exactly two buckets: active students with billing follow-up versus those clear.

It splits active students between those flagged for billing follow-up and those considered clear.

The split uses the same per-student debt signal computed when this page loads (aligned with the Fees matrix): students marked with debt count toward follow-up needed.
health.chartHelpEngagementBody:
Three separate columns compare unrelated magnitudes (different units); column height is the raw count—do not add them as if they were one total.

Engagement points sums profiles.engagement_points for every active student in this section.

Material opens counts portal user_events in roughly the last 30 days (UTC) for those students whose event entity starts with the material prefix.

Learning area events counts user_events for the learning-route entity in the same rolling window. See the note above the charts about the 30-day window.
health.chartHelpAssessmentsBody:
Two horizontal bars share one numeric axis so you can compare published grade rows with pending slots at a glance.

Published grades counts enrollment_assessment_grades rows with status published for active enrollments in this section.

Pending slots is max(expected minus published, 0), where expected is cohort assessment definitions for the parent cohort times active students when both are positive.

Cohort assessment definitions are counted from the cohort assessments catalog.
health.chartHelpReadinessBody:
Two simple columns show how many route steps sit in each readiness flag (needs support versus teacher override).

Each column counts readiness rows on the learning route assigned to this section, computed when the route workspace loads.

These are instructional flags on the route, not attendance.

In free flow or when no route is assigned, counts are usually zero.
